summary: The test suite covers all key requirements well: status classification (Expired/Warning/OK), report grouping, summary counts, configurable warning window, both output formats (JSON and Markdown), file loading, and error handling. Fixtures use a fixed reference date which avoids time-dependent flakiness, and Pester idioms (BeforeAll, Context, $TestDrive) are used correctly. Rigor is strong but has a few gaps: the exact boundary condition where DaysUntilExpiry equals WarningDays is never tested (is 7 days warning or OK?), RequiredBy empty-array handling is untested, JSON/Markdown output tests don't verify RequiredBy values are serialized correctly, row-level urgency values in the Markdown table are not asserted, and the CLI entry point (Invoke-SecretRotationValidator.ps1) has no coverage at all. Design is clean — shared fixtures are factored into BeforeAll blocks, tests are independent, and names are descriptive — though the Format-RotationReport describe shares a single pre-built report object across all contexts which could mask isolation issues. Overall a trustworthy suite that would catch most regressions in core logic.
